description_long: This object is a pair of pliers, constructed from a dark, metallic material with a robust and utilitarian design. The handles are long, straight, and taper slightly towards the ends, providing leverage for gripping and manipulating objects. The jaws are rounded and form a circular opening with a central gap, suitable for gripping or cutting wires and other cylindrical items. The pivot point is a single, solid joint, allowing the two arms to move in opposition and apply force at the jaws. The surface appears smooth and slightly glossy, with no visible insulation or ergonomic padding on the handles. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, emphasizing its functional, industrial character.
description: A sturdy, dark metallic pair of pliers with long, straight handles and rounded jaws, featuring a single pivot joint and a smooth, glossy finish.
description_view_0: Angled view showing the circular jaws and the full length of the straight handles.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the parallel alignment of the handles and the rounded jaw opening.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the open ends of the handles and the back of the jaw mechanism.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, displaying the solid pivot joint and the overall slim profile of the tool.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight, uninsulated metal handles with a slight taper.
attribute_jaw_design: Rounded, circular jaws with a central gap, suitable for gripping or cutting; lacks serrations or notches.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Single fixed pivot joint, non-adjustable, allowing standard opening and closing motion.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with no visible texturing or ergonomic grip pattern.
